top_stories.title: Entra passkey enrollment vishing targets Microsoft 365 users
top_stories.summary: A threat actor is conducting vishing (voice phishing) campaigns targeting Microsoft 365 users across multiple sectors, tricking them into enrolling attacker-controlled passkeys in Microsoft Entra. The attack leverages fake security requests delivered via phone calls to manipulate users into adding malicious passkeys to their accounts, compromising authentication security.

top_stories.title: Extortion crew hijacks Microsoft 365 accounts via fake passkey setup
top_stories.summary: A cyber extortion group called Pink is compromising Microsoft 365 accounts by using vishing calls to trick employees into enrolling attacker-controlled passkeys through fake Microsoft Entra enrollment pages. The attack involves social engineering where attackers impersonate IT staff and direct victims to a malicious subdomain to complete a fraudulent passkey setup, granting the attackers persistent account access.

top_stories.title: Delinea Achieves FedRAMP High Authorization for Secret Server
top_stories.summary: Delinea has achieved FedRAMP High Authorization to Operate (ATO) for its PAM solution, Secret Server. This authorization allows Delinea to provide its privileged access management platform to U.S. federal agencies handling highly sensitive data at the FedRAMP High impact level.

top_stories.title: Barracuda adds PAM and identity protection with Evo Security acquisition
top_stories.summary: Barracuda Networks has acquired Evo Security to expand its identity security capabilities. The deal adds privileged access management (PAM), access control, identity protection, and identity threat detection and response to Barracuda's BarracudaONE platform.

top_stories.title: AI Agents Are a New Kind of Identity & Most Organizations Aren't Ready
top_stories.summary: The article addresses the emerging challenge of managing AI agents as a distinct identity type within IAM frameworks. It argues that treating AI agents like traditional service accounts or API tokens is insufficient and that organizations require a fundamentally different identity management approach for agentic systems.

news.title: AWS gives its ERP agent deny-by-default rules and a separate identity
news.summary: AWS has released an ERP automation agent that incorporates deny-by-default access rules and a separate dedicated identity for the agent. The design gives the agentic AI least-privilege access controls and an isolated identity, reflecting IAM principles applied to non-human/machine identities in agentic AI systems.

news.title: Only 28% of financial workforce MFA is phishing-resistant
news.summary: A new Secret Double Octopus report finds that only 28% of MFA implementations in financial sector workforces are phishing-resistant, with password plus OTP remaining the most common authentication method. Passwords continue to be part of many authentication flows in financial organizations, leaving them vulnerable to phishing and credential theft attacks.

news.title: AI Gateways Offer Attackers the Keys to the Kingdom
news.summary: A cryptomining incident demonstrated how AI gateways can be exploited to gain unauthorized access to AI models, cloud infrastructure, and IAM data. The attack highlights how AI gateway components can serve as an attack vector exposing identity and access management systems to compromise.

news.title: New Helix vishing group emerges in SharePoint data theft attacks
news.summary: A new threat group called Helix has emerged, using identity-focused attack techniques including vishing, device code phishing, and MFA abuse to steal data from SharePoint environments. The group's tactics specifically target authentication mechanisms and identity-based access pathways to conduct data extortion.

news.title: New Forg365 phishing platform uses AI to target Microsoft 365 accounts
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called Forg365 has emerged, targeting Microsoft 365 accounts using adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) and device code phishing techniques combined with AI-generated lures. The platform bypasses traditional authentication controls by intercepting session tokens, posing a direct threat to Microsoft 365 identity and access security.

news.title: Vectogate debuts platform to secure and govern autonomous AI agents
news.summary: Vectogate has launched an AI governance platform in private beta that provides centralized control over autonomous AI agents accessing sensitive data and internal business systems. The platform targets the identity and access governance challenges that arise as AI agents operate with increasing autonomy within enterprise environments.

news.title: npm 12 Disables Install Scripts by Default to Reduce Supply Chain Risk
news.summary: GitHub released npm version 12, which disables install scripts by default and deprecates granular access tokens (GATs) that could bypass two-factor authentication. The 2FA-related change addresses a specific authentication bypass vector in the npm package registry ecosystem.

news.title: Keeper Security surpasses $225M in ARR with transformative growth and is emerging as the market standard for AI-native identity security
news.summary: Keeper Security announced it has surpassed $225 million in annual recurring revenue, positioning itself as a growing player in the identity security market. The company cited product-market fit in agentic AI, expansion of machine identities, and related threats as key growth drivers, with a stated path toward $1 billion ARR and a public offering.

news.title: Lone Attacker Uses AI to Breach AWS Cloud Environment in 72 Hours
news.summary: A lone attacker breached an AWS cloud environment within 72 hours by chaining stolen credentials with AI workflow exploits and cloud misconfigurations. The attack involved credential theft and exploitation of access control weaknesses to compromise a large Amazon customer, culminating in an extortion attempt.

news.title: 3 Ways AI Powers Service Desk Attacks and How to Prevent Them
news.summary: Specops Software has outlined three ways AI is enhancing service desk impersonation attacks, making them more convincing, personalized, and scalable. The article covers practical countermeasures focused on identity verification and onboarding processes at the service desk level.

news.title: The Verification Step Is the New ATO Battleground in 2026
news.summary: An article analyzes the evolving account takeover (ATO) threat landscape, arguing that as passkeys and stronger authentication become mainstream, attackers are shifting focus to the identity verification step itself rather than credential theft. The piece examines how the verification and account recovery processes are becoming the new primary battleground for ATO attacks in 2026.

news.title: What's the best way to do authentication in modern applications
news.summary: The article addresses authentication token storage approaches for modern applications, targeting frontend developers. It compares different methods for handling login tokens without focusing on specific IAM products or enterprise identity standards.

news.title: Quest Software Strengthens Identity Security and Resilience for the AI Era, Achieving FedRAMP® High Authorized Certification for Identity Defense and Identity Recovery
news.summary: Quest Software has achieved FedRAMP High Authorized Certification for its Identity Defense and Identity Recovery solutions. This certification allows federal and regulated organizations to use Quest's identity security and resilience tools at the highest level of government-grade security assurance.

news.title: SailPoint、「Agentic Acceleration」を発表、時間を要するクラウド モダナイゼーションを、迅速なシステム導入へと変革
news.summary: SailPoint announced a new initiative called "Agentic Acceleration" aimed at transforming cloud modernization for identity security deployments. The announcement, originally released in the US on June 16, 2026, focuses on accelerating system implementation that traditionally requires significant time investment.

news.title: Big Brand Jobs Scam Targets Marketing Pros' Google Accounts
news.summary: A phishing campaign is targeting marketing professionals with the goal of stealing Google account credentials. The attack uses nested redirects and other evasion techniques to bypass detection systems.

news.title: DEBULL Tooling Abuses Microsoft Device-Code Flow to Target M365 Accounts
news.summary: A phishing campaign dubbed DEBULL has been observed abusing Microsoft's device-code authentication flow to compromise Microsoft 365 accounts, active from late June through early July 2026. The attack bypasses traditional credential phishing by using collaboration-themed lures to direct victims through the legitimate Microsoft device login process, avoiding fake login pages while still hijacking account access.

news.title: Court Filing Reveals Windows Device ID Helped FBI Trace Alleged Scattered Spider Hacker
news.summary: A federal complaint reveals that the FBI used a persistent Windows device ID to trace an alleged Scattered Spider hacker, Peter Stokes, to a network intrusion at a luxury jewelry retailer. Microsoft records linked the device ID to both the attacker's access account during the May 2025 breach and to online accounts attributed to the 19-year-old suspect.

news.title: Writer AI Flaw Could Let Agent Previews Leak Session Tokens Across Tenants
news.summary: A critical session isolation vulnerability dubbed "WriteOut" was discovered in Writer, an enterprise AI platform, allowing cross-tenant session token leakage via a one-click attack. The flaw, now patched, could have allowed an unauthenticated outsider to compromise any Writer AI tenant by stealing session tokens.

news.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of SailPoint, Inc. - SAIL
news.summary: Pomerantz Law Firm has announced an investigation into claims on behalf of SailPoint investors trading under the NASDAQ ticker SAIL. The notice advises investors to contact the firm but does not specify the nature of the claims being investigated.

news.title: Keeper Security Extends Agentic AI Governance to Endpoint Privilege Manager
news.summary: Keeper Security has extended its agentic AI governance capabilities to its Endpoint Privilege Manager, enabling discovery and governance of AI agents on employee workstations and endpoints. The update introduces access policies and elevation controls for AI agents operating at the endpoint level, framed within Keeper's zero-trust and zero-knowledge identity security platform.

news.title: Microsoft to enable Windows settings backup by default for enterprise devices
news.summary: Microsoft will enable Windows settings backup and restore by default in Windows 11 version 26H2 for enterprise devices joined to Microsoft Entra. The change transitions the feature from opt-in to default-enabled on Entra-joined systems.

news.title: Privileged Access Management Market Size to Hit $22.93 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
news.summary: SNS Insider projects the global Privileged Access Management market will reach $22.93 billion by 2035, with the U.S. segment accounting for $6.02 billion and Europe $5.01 billion. Growth drivers cited include rising cyberattacks, GDPR compliance requirements, enterprise cloud adoption, and demand for zero trust frameworks.

news.title: Microsoft to enable Windows settings backup by default for orgs
news.summary: Microsoft will enable Windows settings backup and restore by default on Microsoft Entra-joined and hybrid-joined enterprise systems following an upgrade to Windows 11 26H2. The change applies specifically to devices managed through Microsoft Entra, affecting enterprise IT configurations tied to that identity platform.

news.title: Identifying and Using OnPremisesImmutableId from Azure AD Connect Synchronization Errors
news.summary: The article addresses resolving Azure AD Connect "Duplicate Attribute" synchronization errors by extracting and using the OnPremisesImmutableId directly from sync error data. The technique applies to hybrid identity management environments where on-premises Active Directory is synchronized with Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD).

news.title: Your company already adopted AI and nobody is governing access
news.summary: Antoine Berton, CTO at Elba Security, discusses ungoverned access created by AI tool adoption in a Help Net Security video. The core issue addressed is that employees connecting AI tools to corporate systems create access relationships that fall outside traditional identity governance frameworks.

news.title: Microsoft wants to keep your AI agents from going rogue
news.summary: Microsoft has introduced Microsoft Execution Containers (MXC), a policy-driven execution layer designed to control and constrain AI agent behavior on Windows and WSL platforms. The technology enforces authorization boundaries for AI agents, directly relating to access management and zero trust principles as applied to agentic AI systems.

news.title: What does zero trust mean for mobile devices in the enterprise?
news.summary: The article examines the application of zero trust principles to mobile device security in enterprise environments. It addresses how organizations can protect corporate resources as mobile devices interact with them, given the rise in mobile threats.

news.title: Enforce least-privilege authorization in multi-agent AI chains using Cedar
news.summary: The article covers enforcing least-privilege authorization in multi-agent AI systems using Cedar, Amazon's policy language. It focuses on preventing authorization scope creep in multi-hop agent delegation chains, a directly relevant access control and authorization topic for IAM professionals.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to an August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and phishing to bypass MFA and gain unauthorized access to corporate systems.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords, briefly seizing high-profile accounts including the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force Chief Master Sergeant. Instructions for the social engineering technique were circulated on Telegram, enabling attackers to bypass normal account authentication and recovery processes.
